title: Lophanthus oxyodontus
taxonomy: Lophanthus oxyodontus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Lophanthus, which consists of several species of shrubs and herbs native to the western United States and Canada. The species is further classified as a type of subshrub or shrubby herb. The plant is native to dry, rocky areas and is often found in association with other drought-tolerant species. Further classification of Lophanthus oxyodontus may also include its taxonomy within the broader plant kingdom.
uses: Lophanthus oxyodontus, also known as the 'woolly blue-cushion', is a plant native to North America. Lophanthus oxyodontus has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and infection prevention. intro:
